news: FDA granted Fast Track designation to KT-621 for moderate to severe eosinophilic asthma, adding to existing AD Fast Track designation.

changes.reason: Fast Track designation for asthma validates FDA's view of KT-621's potential across Type 2 inflammation, reducing regulatory risk
Fast Track designation typically accelerates review timeline and provides more regulatory guidance, improving approval probability
Dual Fast Track designations (AD + asthma) strengthen conviction in regulatory pathway for broad Type 2 inflammation franchise
Even in bear case, Fast Track designation reduces regulatory execution risk for asthma indication

news: Revolution Medicines announces $1 billion financing ($750M equity + $250M convertibles) following positive Phase 3 RASolute 302 data showing daraxonrasib nearly doubled overall survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer.

changes.reason: $1B financing adds to existing $2.2B cash position
$750M equity offering at current price creates ~15% dilution
Equity dilution compounds existing bear case concerns
Even in bull case, equity dilution impacts per-share value
Equity dilution affects all scenarios with share issuance

news: CRISPR Therapeutics gained 7% in a day and 12.5% over the month with strong Casgevy launch momentum showing over $100M in sales and six upcoming data readouts planned.

changes.reason: Strong Casgevy sales momentum above $100M confirms improved market penetration trajectory
Outperforming commercial launch validates higher penetration potential in bull case

news: Kelun-Biotech reported Phase II data on sac-TMT as maintenance treatment in BRCA wild-type, second-line platinum-sensitive recurrent ovarian cancer at the 2026 Society of Gynecologic Oncology Annual Meeting.

changes.reason: New ovarian cancer data expands sac-TMT addressable market beyond currently approved indications
Strong ovarian cancer data supports expanded label potential for sac-TMT globally
Ovarian cancer maintenance setting represents significant additional market opportunity for TROP2 ADC

news: FILSPARI received full FDA approval for FSGS on April 14, 2026, making it the first FDA-approved medicine for this condition and expanding beyond IgA nephropathy into a second rare kidney disease.

changes.reason: FDA approval already achieved - probability of success remains at 100%
Full FDA approval confirmed - approval probability remains at 100%
